CTSW101:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW101
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
From the AMQP test interface send an AMQP text message to the
IUT. The AMQP text message shall address a remote AMHS user and
consist of AMQP priority property with default value (4) and specify
a text payload within the AMQP property amqp-value.
Check that the AMHS Test Tool receives an AMHS message from the
IUT via the AMHS transfer port. In particular, verify that:
• the payload of the received AMHS message contains the text
send from the AMQP test interface.
• the AMQP default priority has been correctly translated to the
element ATS-message-priority or the IPM precedence and to
the AMHS message transfer envelope priority depending on
“ATSMHS Service Level” parameter.
• the filing time corresponding with the creation time of the
AMQP message is correctly inserted and converted to format
DDhhmm in the ATS-message-header or in the
Authorization-time if the message contains IHE depending on
“ATSMHS Service Level” parameter.
• the addressee indicator of the application property
amhs_recipients is correctly translated in the corresponding
AMHS OR address and entered as primary-recipient in the
IPM heading and as recipient-name in the MTE.
• the trace-information or the element internal-trace
information element are in accordance with sections 4.5.4.274.5.4.31 of The following components of the element per-message-indicators
are present in the MTE:
• alternate-recipient-allowed with the abstract value “alternaterecipient-allowed”
• disclosure-of-other-recipients takes the abstract value
“disclosure-of-other-recipients-prohibited”
• implicit-conversion-prohibited takes the abstract value
“implicit-conversion-allowed”
• content-return-request takes the abstract value “contentreturn-not-requested”
The following components of the element extensions are not
present or are present in the MTE with their default values:
• recipient-reassignment-prohibited with the abstract value
“recipient-reassignment-allowed”
• dl-expansion-prohibited takes the abstract value “dlexpansion-allowed”
• conversion-with-loss-prohibited takes the abstract value
“conversion-with-loss-allowed”
The components of the element per-recipient-indicators are
generated taking the following abstract-values:
• “responsible” for the responsibility element
• “non-delivery-report” for the originating-MTA-report-request
element
• “non-delivery-report” for the originator-report-request
element
From the AMQP test interface send a AMQP binary message. The
AMQP message shall address a remote AMHS user and consist of
AMQP priority property with default value (4) and specify binary
payload within AMQP data property.
Check the IPMs that the AMHS Test Tool receives an AMHS
message from the IUT via the AMHS transfer port. In particular,
verify that:
• the payload of the received AMHS message contains the
binary content send from the AMQP test interface.
• the AMQP default priority has been correctly translated to the
element ATS-message-priority or the IPM precedence and to
the AMHS message transfer envelope priority depending on
“ATSMHS Service Level” parameter.
• the filing time corresponding with the creation time of the
AMQP message is correctly inserted and converted to format
DDhhmm in the ATS-message-header or in the
Authorization-time if the message contains IHE depending on
“ATSMHS Service Level” parameter.
• the addressee indicator of the application property
amhs_recipients is correctly translated in the corresponding
AMHS OR address and entered as primary-recipient in the
IPM heading and as recipient-name in the MTE.

the trace-information or the element internal-trace
information element are in accordance with sections 4.5.4.274.5.4.31 of 4.5.4.20 private-domain-identifier
4.5.4.21 disclosure-of-other-recipients
4.5.4.22 implicit-conversion-prohibited
4.5.4.23 alternate-recipient-allowed
4.5.4.24 content-return-request
4.5.4.25 AMHS management domain
4.5.4.27 global-domain-identifier in the trace-information or in the
internal-trace-information
4.5.4.28 arrival-time in the first element of trace-information or of
internal-trace-information
4.5.4.29 routing-action
4.5.4.30 mta-name
4.5.4.31
4.5.1.9
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| priority | 4 | ATS-message-priority (precedence) | 4.5.1.1, Table 9 |
| amqp-value | <Text Payload> | ATS-message-text (ia5-text) | 4.5.1.4, 4.5.2.14 |
| data | <Binary Payload> | file-transfer-body-part | 4.5.1.4, 4.5.2.13 |
| amhs_recipients | <Recipient Address> | primary-recipient | 4.5.2.9 |
| (creation-time) | <Filing Time> | ATS-message-Filing-Time | 4.5.2.10 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW102:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW102
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
From the SWIM Test Tool send a sequence of AMQP messages to the
IUT addressing an AMHS user.
Text messages
The following messages shall contain <text/plain; charset=”utf-8”>
content-type.
•
•
•
•
•

The first AMQP message shall have 10 as priority.
Optionally, the second AMQP message shall have empty
message-id element.
Optionally, the third AMQP message shall have 0 as creationtime.
The fourth AMQP message shall have empty amqp-value.
The fifth AMQP message shall have empty amhs_recipients
element.

Optionally, another AMQP message shall have empty content-type
element.

Binary messages
The following messages shall contain <application/octet-stream>
content-type.
•
•
•

•
•

The fourth AMQP message shall have empty data element.
The fifth AMQP message shall have an address of more than
eight letters as amhs_recipients element.
Optionally, another AMQP message shall have empty content-type
element.
Verify that all messages are rejected, logged, and reported to the
Control Position.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | Expected AMHS Action | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| priority | 10 | Reject, Log, Report | 4.5.1.1 |
| message-id | (empty) | Reject, Log, Report | 4.5.1.2 |
| creation-time | 0 | Reject, Log, Report | 4.5.1.3 |
| amqp-value | (empty) | Reject, Log, Report | 4.5.1.4 |
| data | (empty) | Reject, Log, Report | 4.5.1.4 |
| amhs_recipients | (empty or >8 char) | Reject, Log, Report | 4.5.1.5 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW103:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW103
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
From the AMQP test interface send a sequence of AMQP messages to
the IUT.
The sequence of AMQP messages shall address a remote AMHS user
and consist of seven messages.
ATSMHS Service Level – Basic
For the first message, the ATSMHS Service Level parameter shall be
set to “basic”.
Then AMQP elements susceptible to being translated using extended
or basic ATSMHS service level will be mapped to basic service level.
Check the IPM that the AMHS Test Tool receives. Verify that the
IUT has converted the message correctly accordingly:
•
•
•

•

amhs_ats_ohi shall be conveyed as the value of the optional
heading information element in the ATS-message-OptionalHeading-Info
amhs_ats_ft shall be conveyed as the value of the ATSmessage-Filing-Time
amhs_ats_pri shall be conveyed as the value of the priorityindicator of the ATS-message-priority element of the AMHS
message, in compliance with the mapping specified in the
Table 9 of the amhs_ats_ohi shall be conveyed as the value of the
originators-reference of the IPM heading.
amhs_ats_ft shall be conveyed as the value of the element
Authorization-time if the message contains IHE and converted
to format: DDhhmm.
amhs_ats_pri shall be mapped into one of the authorized
values of the precedence element of the recipient extensions of
the recipient-specifier as specified in the Table 9 of the EUR
Doc 047.
the value of precedence-policy-identifier shall be 1.3.27.8.0.0

ATSMHS Service Level – Content based.
For the fourth message, the ATSMHS Service Level parameter shall
be set to “content-based” and content-type of the AMQP message
shall be “<application/octet-stream>”
Check the IPM that the AMHS Test Tool receives. Verify that the
IUT has converted the messages correctly according to the test
“ATSMHS Service Level – Extended.”
For the fifth message, the ATSMHS Service Level parameter shall be
set to “content-based” and content-type of the AMQP message shall
be “<text/plain; charset=”utf-8”>”
Check the IPM that the AMHS Test Tool receives. Verify that the
IUT has converted the messages correctly according to the test
“ATSMHS Service Level – Basic.”
ATSMHS Service Level – Recipient based
For the sixth message, ATSMHS Service Level parameter shall be set
to “recipient-based” and all the recipients of the converted AMHS
message shall support the extended format.
Check the IPM that the AMHS Test Tool receives. Verify that the
IUT has converted the messages correctly according to the test
“ATSMHS Service Level – Extended.”
For the seventh message, ATSMHS Service Level parameter shall be
set to “recipient-based” and all the recipients, except for one, of the
converted AMHS message shall support the extended format.
Check the IPM that the AMHS Test Tool receives. Verify that the
IUT has converted the messages correctly according to the test
“ATSMHS Service Level – Basic.”
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value / Condition | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------------------|----------------------|-----------------|
| Service Level | basic | ATS-Message-Header | 3.3.3, 4.5.3.9 |
| Service Level | extended | originators-reference, Authorization-time, precedence | 3.3.3, 4.5.3.7 |
| Service Level | content-based | (text->basic, bin->reject/ext) | 3.3.3 |
| Service Level | recipient-based | (mixed capabilities) | 3.3.3 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW104:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW104
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Convert incoming AMQP messages with different priorities to
AMHS
This test is successful, if the IUT converts an AMQP message with
minimal required information to an AMHS message (IPM) correctly.
From the AMQP test interface send a sequence of nine AMQP
messages to the IUT. The sequence of AMQP messages shall address
a remote AMHS user and consist of ten messages, one for each
AMQP priority, i.e., according to the Table 9 of the EUR Doc 047
and specifying a text payload within the AMQP application data
amqp-value.
Check the IPMs that the AMHS Test Tool receives from the IUT via
the AMHS transfer port. Verify that the IUT has converted the
priority of the messages correctly according to Table 9 of the EUR
Doc 047.
From the AMQP test interface send a sequence of five AMQP
messages to the IUT. The sequence of AMQP messages shall address
a remote AMHS user and consist of five messages, containing default
AMQP priority (4), and one of each of the possible values of the
AMQP application property amhs_ats_pri (SS,DD,FF,GG,KK).
Check the IPMs that the AMHS Test Tool receives from the IUT via
the AMHS transfer port. Verify that the IUT has converted messages
priority correctly according to Table 9 of the EUR Doc 047.
From the AMQP test interface send a sequence of four AMQP
messages to the IUT. The sequence of AMQP messages shall address
a remote AMHS user and consist of four messages, containing AMQP
priority 1, and one of each of the possible values of the AMQP
application property amhs_ats_pri (SS,DD,FF,GG).
From the AMQP test interface send an AMQP message to the IUT.
The AMQP message shall address a remote AMHS user and consist
of one message, containing default AMQP priority 9 and AMQP
application property amhs_ats_pri with value KK.
Check the IPMs that the AMHS Test Tool receives from the IUT via
the AMHS transfer port. Verify that the IUT has considered that
AMQP application property amhs_ats_pri takes precedence over
AMQP priority value. That is, verify that the IUT has converted
AMQP application property amhs_ats_pri according to Table 9 of the
EUR Doc 047.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| priority / amhs_ats_pri | 0-9 / SS,DD,FF,GG,KK | ATS-message-priority | 4.5.2.2, Table 9 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW105:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW105
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Convert incoming AMHS aware AMQP messages containing
filing time to AMHS
This test is successful, if the IUT converts an AMQP message with
minimal required information to an AMHS message (IPM) correctly.
From the AMQP test interface send a sequence of AMQP messages to
the IUT. The sequence of AMQP messages shall address a remote
AMHS user and consist of:
•
•

The 1st AMQP message shall have empty amhs_ats_ft
application property.
The 2nd AMQP message shall have “250102” as amhs_ats_ft
application property.

Check the IPMs that the AMHS Test Tool receives from the IUT via
the AMHS transfer port. Verify that:
• For the first message, the filing time corresponding to the
creation time of the AMQP message is correctly inserted and
converted to DDhhmm format in the ATS-message-header or
in the Authorization-time depending on “ATSMHS Service
Level” parameter, as specified in EUR Doc 047 – 4.5.2.10.1.b
• For the second message, the filing time from amhs_ats_ft
application property shall be conveyed as the value of the
ATS-message-Filing-Time in the ATS-message-header or in
the Authorization-time depending on “ATSMHS Service
Level” parameter as specified in EUR Doc 047 – 4.5.2.10.1.b
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| amhs_ats_ft | 250102 (or empty) | ATS-message-Filing-Time | 4.5.2.10 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW106:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW106
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Convert an AMHS aware AMQP message containing optional
heading information
This test is successful, if the IUT converts an AMQP message
containing optional heading information (OHI) correctly into an
AMHS message (IPM).
From the AMQP test interface send a sequence of AMQP messages
to the IUT. The sequence of AMQP messages shall address a remote
AMHS user and consist of:
•
•
•
•
•
•

The 1st AMQP message shall have “4” as priority and contain
amhs_ats_ohi text of less than 53 characters.
The 2nd AMQP message shall have “4” as priority and
contain amhs_ats_ohi text of exactly 53 characters.
The 3rd AMQP message shall have “4” as priority and
contain amhs_ats_ohi text of more than 53 characters.
The 4th AMQP message shall have “6” as priority and contain
amhs_ats_ohi text of less than 48 characters.
The 5th AMQP message shall have “6” as priority and contain
amhs_ats_ohi text of exactly 48 characters.
The 6th AMQP message shall have “6” as priority and contain
amhs_ats_ohi text of more than 48 characters.

Verify that the IUT has converted the messages correctly to extended
AMHS messages with appropriate originators-reference element.
Check that for the 3rd and 6th messages, amhs_ats_ohi text of AMHS
message has been trimmed down, to 53 for the case of the 3rd
message and to 48 for the 6th message.
Optionally, if Basic ATSMHS service level is implemented by the
IUT, then repeat this sequence forcing the generation of ATSMessage-Header and verifying that the IUT has conveyed appropriate
value of amhs_ats_ohi text to the ATS-Message-Header.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| amhs_ats_ohi | <53, =53, >53 chars | originators-reference (trimmed) | 4.5.2.11 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW107:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW107
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Convert an AMQP message containing subject
This test is successful, if the IUT converts an AMQP message subject
correctly into an AMHS message (IPM).
From the AMQP test interface send a sequence of AMQP messages
to the IUT. The sequence of AMQP messages shall address a remote
AMHS user and consist of:
•
•
•
•

The 1st AMQP message shall include the AMQP subject
property with size value exceeding 128 characters and empty
AMQP amhs_subject application property
The 2nd AMQP message shall include the AMQP subject
property and empty AMQP amhs_subject application
property.
The 3rd AMQP message shall have empty AMQP subject
property and “Subject example” as AMQP amhs_subject
application property.
The 4th AMQP message shall have “subject from properties”
as AMQP subject property and “subject from application
property field” as AMQP amhs_subject application property.

Verify that the IUT has converted the messages correctly.
Check that for the 1st AMHS message the property subject was
trimmed to 128 characters and mapped to the IPM element subject.
Check that for the 2nd AMHS message the property subject was
mapped to the IPM element subject.
Check that for 3rd and 4th message, the application property
amhs_subject was mapped to the IPM element subject.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| subject / amhs_subject | >128 chars, empty | subject (trimmed to 128) | 4.5.2.3 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW108:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW108
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Incoming AMQP message with known originator indicator
This test is successful, if the IUT correctly converts an AMQP
message containing a known originator into an AMHS message
(IPM).
From the AMQP test interface send an AMQP message to the IUT.
The AMQP message shall contain an originator indicator with an 8
letters address which is known in the IUT and that can be converted.
Check the IPM that the AMHS Test Tool receives. Verify that the
IUT has converted the message correctly accordingly:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| amhs_originator | 8-char known address | originator (this-IPM) | 4.5.2.12, 4.5.3.5 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW109:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW109
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Incoming AMQP message with unknown originator indicator
This test is successful, if the IUT logs and reports the AMQP
message to the Control Position, if during the conversion process the
translation of the originator indicator fails. In this case, the default
originator shall be used.
From the AMQP test interface send an AMQP message to the IUT.
The AMQP message shall contain an originator indicator which is
unknown in the IUT and therefore it cannot be converted.
Verify that the IUT converts and sends the message to the AMHS
Test Tool using the default originator specified in the AMHS/SWIM
Gateway’s configuration parameters.
Check that this situation has been logged and reported to the Control
Position.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | Expected AMHS Action | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| amhs_originator | unknown address | Replaced with Default Originator, Logged | 4.5.2.12 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW110:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW110
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Reject an AMQP message with unsupported content-type
This test is successful, if the IUT’s gateway component rejects an
incoming message if it does not meet with the following assertions:
• content-type contains the value “<application/octetstream>”, the data element is present, and the amqp-value
element is not present.
• content-type contains the value “<text/plain; charset=”utf8”>”, the amqp-value element is present, and the data
element is not present
From the AMQP test interface send a sequence of messages to the
IUT addressed to an AMHS user recipient.
The messages shall have different values for the content-type:
•
•
•
•
•
•

The 1st message shall contain a content-type value
“<application/octet-stream>”, while amqp-value and data
shall be empty.
The 2nd message shall contain a content-type value
“<text/plain; charset=”utf-8”>”, amqp-value shall be
empty, and data shall contain binary data.
The 3rd message shall contain a content-type value
“<application/octet-stream>”, amqp-value shall be empty,
and data shall contain binary data.
The 4th message shall contain a content-type value
“<text/plain; charset=”utf-8”>”, amqp-value shall be
present and data shall be empty.
Optionally, the 5th message shall contain a content-type
value “<text/plain; charset=”utf-16”>”
Optionally, the 6th message shall contain a content-type
value “<text/plain; charset=”utf-8”>”, amqp-value shall
be present and data shall contain binary data.

Verify that the IUT accepts and converts the 3rd and 4th messages,
but rejects 1st, 2nd, 5th and 6th messages.
Check that this situation has been logged and reported to Control
Position.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | Expected AMHS Action | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| content-type | mismatch with data/value| Reject, Log | 4.5.1.6 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW111:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW111
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Check if the size in bytes of the payload of an AMQP
message exceeds the maximum configured
This test is successful, if the IUT rejects a received AMQP message
if the size of the payload in bytes of the message exceeds the
maximum value specified in the AMHS/SWIM Gateway’s
configuration parameter “Maximum message data size.”
From the AMQP test interface send three AMQP messages to the
IUT transfer port. The messages shall address an AMHS user.
a) Send one AMQP message, the size of the amqp-value shall
not exceed the maximum value configured.
b) Send one more AMQP message, the size of the payload in
bytes for data shall not exceed the maximum value
configured.
Verify that these two messages are received at the AMHS test
interface.
c) Send one AMQP message, the size of the amqp-value shall
exceed the maximum value configured.
d) Send one more AMQP message, the size of the payload in
bytes for data shall exceed the maximum value configured.
Verify that for the last two messages the IUT does not convert the
AMQP messages into AMHS format, but rejects the messages, logs,
and reports the situation to the Control Position.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | Expected AMHS Action | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| Payload Size | > Max Configured Bytes | Reject, Log | 4.5.1.7 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW112:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW112
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Check if an AMQP message addresses more AMHS users than
the maximum configured
This test is successful, if the IUT rejects a received AMQP message
addressing a number of message recipients, which the ITCU is
responsible for conveyance, that exceeds the maximum value defined
in the AMHS/SWIM Gateway’s configuration parameter “Maximum
message number of recipients.”
From the AMQP test interface send two AMQP messages to the IUT.
a) Send one AMQP message with 512 AMHS recipients. The
“Maximum message number of recipients” parameter is set to
512.
Verify that this message is received at the AMHS test interface.
b) Send one more AMQP message with 513 recipients. The
“Maximum message number of recipients” parameter is set to
512.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | Expected AMHS Action | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| amhs_recipients | > Max Num Configured | Reject, Log | 4.5.1.8 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW113:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW113
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Incoming receipt and non-receipt notification (RN and NRN)
This test is successful, if the IUT reports to its control position and
stores the message, if it receives an IPN containing a NRN or RN
addressed to an AMQP consumer.
From the AMQP test interface send two AMQP messages with
priority “6” to the IUT. These messages shall address an AMQP
consumer and be converted by the IUT into AMHS format and sent
to the AMHS Test Tool.
Remove Message 1 from the AMHS Test Tool
For the first one, upon reception of the AMHS message, the AMHS
Test Tool returns an NRN.
Verify that the IUT behaves as specified in section 4.4.7.3 of EUR
Doc 047, i.e.:
• logs the situation and reports to Control Position, and
• stores the message for appropriate processing at the Control
Position.
For the second one, upon reception of the AMHS message the AMHS
Test Tool returns a RN.
Check the element notification-requests in the AMHS message. The
values “RN” and “NRN” shall be taken simultaneously by this
element.
Verify that the IUT behaves the same way as mentioned before and:
• logs the situation and reports to Control Position, and
• stores the message for appropriate processing at the Control
Position.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | Expected AMHS Action | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| amhs_notification_request | rn, nrn | Log, Store for Control Position | 4.5.3.4, 4.4.7.3 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW114:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW114
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Incoming non-delivery reports (l)
This test is successful, if the IUT logs and reports to its control
position, if it receives an NDR.
From the AMQP test interface send an AMQP message to the IUT.
The message shall address an AMHS user and be converted by the IUT
into AMHS format.
This message shall be queued in the AMHS Test Tool. After being
queued, it shall be deleted(sending report to originator).
Verify that an NDR is received by the IUT with the following
elements:
• “unable-to-transfer” for the non-delivery-reason-code,
• empty field for the non-delivery-diagnostic-code ,
Check that the IUT logs and reports the situation to the Control
Position.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMHS Event | Generated Report | Gateway Action | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|------------|------------------|----------------|-----------------|
| NDR (non-delivery) | unable-to-transfer | Log, Report to Control Position | 4.4.1.3 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW115:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW115
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
All messages shall contain <text/plain; charset=\”utf-8\”> content
type and:
•
•

•

•

The first AMQP message shall have for amhs_bodypart_type
set to “ia5-text”, amhs_content_encoding set to “IA5” and
amqp-value set to “Lorem ipsum”.
The second AMQP message shall have for
amhs_bodypart_type set to “ia5_text_body_part”,
amhs_content_encoding set to “IA5” and amqp-value set to
“Lorem ipsum i5bpt”.
The third AMQP message shall have for
amhs_bodypart_type set to “general-text-body-part”, amhscontent-encoding set to “ISO-646” and amqp-value set to
“Lorem ipsum 646”.
The fourth AMQP message shall have for
amhs_bodypart_type set to “general-text-body-part”,
amhs_content_encoding set to “ISO- 8859-1” and amqpvalue set to “Lorem ipsum 8859”.

Check messages received at the AMHS interface. Check the correct
format of the AMHS messages according to the Table 10 of EUR
Doc 047.
Verify that the ATS-message-text of the AMHS messages is equal to
the text set in the amqp-value for each message.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| amhs_bodypart_type | ia5-text, general-text | original-encoded-information-types | 4.5.2.4, 4.5.4.7, Table 10 |
| amhs_content_encoding | IA5, ISO-646, ISO-8859-1 | original-encoded-information-types | 4.5.2.4, Table 11 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CTSW116:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CTSW116
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST REQUIREMENT: 
Incoming AMQP binary message with FTBP attributes
This test is successful, if the IUT’s gateway component converts a
received AMQP binary message to an AMHS message (IPM) with a
FTBP and appropriate attributes.
From the SWIM Test Tool send a sequence of AMQP messages
(IPMs) to the IUT addressed to an AMHS user reachable via the
IUT’s gateway.
•

•

The first message shall contain <application/octet-stream>
content-type and an empty amqp-value. Also, data element
shall contain a binary file and the application properties
amhs_ftbp_file_name, amhs_ftbp_object_size and
amhs_ftbp_last_mod.
The second message shall contain the same elements, and
additionally the application property swim_compression shall
contain value “gzip”. In this case, the binary payload shall be
a compressed content.

Check the messages received at AMHS interface. Check the correct
format of the AMHS message, which shall contain a file-transferbody-part.
Verify that AMHS properties related to FTBP are correct:
•
•
•

incomplete-pathname element of the file transfer parameters
of the AMHS message shall be conveyed using
amhs_ftbp_file_name element of AMQP message.
actual-values element of the IPM file transfer parameters of
the AMHS message shall be conveyed using
amhs_ftbp_object_size element of AMQP message.
date-and-time-of-last-modification element of the IPM file
transfer parameters of the AMHS message shall be conveyed
using amhs_ftbp_last_mod element of AMQP message if
present.

In the second message, verify that the payload has been
uncompressed.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PAYLOAD:
| AMQP Property | Value | AMHS (X.400) Element | EUR Doc 047 Ref |
|---------------|-------|----------------------|-----------------|
| amhs_ftbp_file_name | <FileName> | incomplete-pathname (file-transfer-body-part) | 4.5.2.6 |
| amhs_ftbp_object_size | <Size> | actual-values | 4.5.2.7 |
| amhs_ftbp_last_mod | <DDMMYYhhmmssZ> | date-and-time-of-last-modification | 4.5.2.8 |
| swim_compression | gzip | Auto-decompressed by Gateway | 4.5.2 |
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
